definitions.technical: The federal agency that insures bank deposits up to $250,000 per depositor and manages the resolution of failed banks. When a bank fails, the FDIC places it into receivership, taking control of its assets and liabilities.
definitions.lay: The government agency that protects bank depositors by insuring their accounts up to $250,000. When a bank fails, the FDIC steps in to take over and sort out who gets paid. Deposits above the insurance limit may be temporarily frozen.
definitions.parenthetical: (the federal agency insuring bank deposits and managing failed bank resolutions)
definitions.legal_significance: The FDIC's receivership process is central to the comment letter's analysis. Stablecoin reserves held at a failed bank in amounts exceeding the $250,000 insurance limit are subject to receivership delays, as demonstrated when Circle's $3.3 billion at SVB was temporarily inaccessible.
